title: Graduate Student Instructor - SI 650 Information Retrieval
company: School of Information, University of Michigan
points: Facilitate weekly office hours and assist students with coursework and projects.
Grade assignments and provide constructive feedback to enhance student learning.
Collaborate with the lead instructor to develop course materials and improve curriculum.

title: R&D Intern
points: Constructed an OCR pipeline for identifying recovery seed phrases in real-world images by combining image processing, text recognition using PaddleOCR, and phrase validation using a Trie-based matching over the BIP-39 dictionary.
Built a lightweight filtering system using Laplacian blur detection to improve efficiency and increase recall rate by 40%.

title: Research Assistant
points: Evaluated the AI chatbot's performance on the center's website through NLP methods, and analyzed the chatbot usage.

Performed exploratory data analysis (EDA) on post-graduate year medical student training outcome data and trained ML models such as regression and random forest with more than 0.85 accuracy to provide insight on future training.

title: R&D AI Engineer Intern
points: Trained deep learning models with YOLO-v8 and YOLO-v9 frameworks to automate image segmentation, achieving an average MAP of over 0.90 and over 0.95 with image augmentation.
Developed algorithms that check image quality, analyze medical images, and diagnose diseases with the segmentation result, achieving an average accuracy of over 0.85.

title: Software Engineer Intern
company: Institute of Information Science, Academia Sinica
points: Engineered a digital diary application using Android Studio, enabling the collection of clinical data from over 200 patients diagnosed with GERD while integrating an online questionnaire for comprehensive health reporting.
